sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Deportivo Alavés have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, securing 3 wins (including a strong 3-0 away victory) but suffering 2 losses. Real Oviedo, on the other hand, are in poor form, winless in their last 11 matches with no goals scored in their last 7 outings, highlighting a severe lack of attacking threat.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, each team has claimed a narrow victory, with Real Oviedo winning 1-0 at home in January 2023 and Alavés edging a 2-1 win at home in October 2022. Matches between these sides tend to be low-scoring, with under 2.5 goals in 6 of their last 8 meetings.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Alavés, sitting 15th with 18 points, have struggled offensively (14 goals, 17th in the league) but are defensively solid (20 goals conceded, 6th best). Oviedo, languishing in 20th with just 11 points, have the league's worst attack (7 goals) and a leaky defense (26 conceded), underlining their relegation battle.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: Carlos Vicente has been Alavés’ standout performer recently, with 3 goals and 1 assist in the last 5 matches, boasting a 7.3 Sofascore rating. Oviedo lack in-form players, but their defensive structure might rely on David Costas, who has a solid 6.94 average rating this season.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Alavés are likely to dominate possession (average 49.5%) and create chances through final third entries (23.8 per game), while Oviedo’s inability to convert big chances (evident from recent stats) could hinder any counter-attacking threat. Expect Alavés to target late goals, as they’ve scored 30% of their goals in the 76-90 minute range.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Alavés’ superior form and home advantage at Estadio de Mendizorroza, coupled with Oviedo’s dismal scoring record, a home win seems likely. Sofascore’s probabilities (43% home win, 41% draw, 16% away win) align with this, though a draw remains a possibility if Oviedo defend stubbornly.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Deportivo Alavés and Real Oviedo at Estadio de Mendizorroza ended in a goalless draw. Despite the lack of goals, both teams showed intent, with Alavés holding a slight edge in possession and creating a few opportunities. Oviedo, however, looked more dangerous at times, generating several big chances but failing to convert any of them. Both sides hit the woodwork once, underlining how close they came to breaking the deadlock.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attacking momentum
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in attack shifted multiple times throughout the half. Early on, Oviedo dominated with consistent attacking spells, particularly in the opening stages where they pressed high and created sustained pressure. Alavés struggled to get into rhythm initially but started to push back with brief attacking bursts. As the half progressed, Alavés gained more control, especially in the latter stages, stringing together several attacking moves. Oviedo, however, ended the half with a strong surge, showing they still have plenty of threat going forward.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance and key stats
halftimeSections.text: Deportivo Alavés displayed a balanced approach, maintaining slightly more possession and recording a decent number of entries into the final third. Their duel-winning ability was marginally better, which helped them retain control in key areas. Real Oviedo, on the other hand, looked more clinical in attack, creating numerous big chances but lacking the finishing touch, as evidenced by their missed opportunities. Oviedo also earned more corner kicks, showing their ability to push forward and create set-piece situations. Both teams showed defensive resilience, with no goals conceded, but Oviedo's higher expected goals suggest they were closer to scoring.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Alavés, Carles Aleñá stood out with his creative presence in midfield, getting involved in key passes and taking shots on target. Youssef Enriquez Lekhedim also showed promise with his contributions in the final third. On Oviedo's side, Josip Brekalo was a standout performer, heavily involved in creating chances and posing a constant threat. Ilyas Chaira also contributed well with his ability to unlock defenses through key passes. Both teams have players capable of turning the game in their favor in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see both teams push harder for a breakthrough, with Oviedo potentially capitalizing on their attacking intent from the first half. Alavés will need to convert their possession into more clear-cut chances if they are to take the lead. Given Oviedo's higher expected goals and missed big chances, they might have the edge if they can sharpen their finishing. Tactical adjustments could play a big role, and Alavés might look to exploit set-pieces or counter-attacks to shift the momentum. The game remains finely poised, but goals seem inevitable if both teams maintain their attacking hunger.
